reasoning_model_1: Model 1 fails to strictly adhere to the provided script. It adds a fake crying sound 'Wuwuwu' (00:00-00:01) at the beginning and adds the modal particle 'ah' (啊) at the end (00:03), resulting in 'Wuwuwu, my puppy is gone, I am so sad ah'. Regarding the emotion 'sadness', the performance is very unnatural. The 'wuwuwu' sound is reminiscent of a cartoon character pretending to cry or acting cute (撒娇) rather than experiencing genuine grief. The tone during the speech (00:01-00:04) is surprisingly high-pitched and brisk, lacking the heaviness, slower tempo, or trembling voice usually associated with sadness. It sounds more like a playful or performative whine than actual sadness.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 adheres perfectly to the text script '我的小狗不见了，我好难过', with no additions or omissions. The emotional expression is excellent. The speaker adopts a lower pitch and a slower tempo appropriate for sadness. There is a distinct 'choked-up' quality (00:02-00:03) in the voice when saying 'wo hao nan guo' (I am so sad), effectively conveying a sense of holding back tears or genuine distress. The breathing and prosody sound very human and natural, effectively communicating the requested emotion without being exaggerated.
model_comparison: Model 2 is significantly superior to Model 1. First, Model 2 respects the content accuracy requirement, whereas Model 1 hallucinates non-existent text ('Wuwuwu' and 'ah'). Second, in terms of the core paralinguistic feature 'sadness', Model 2 provides a realistic, grounded, and emotionally resonant performance with a choked-up voice. In contrast, Model 1 produces a caricature of sadness that sounds like a cartoon sound effect or someone acting cute, which contradicts the serious nature of the text.
